Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR No. 9711, e-mail: lreding@sftc.org. (L&M302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Joshua D. Franklin of the law offices of Franklin Soto LLP (619-872-2521) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, UMB Bank N.A.

Attorney, Scott W. Wilkinson of the law offices of Davis & Ceriani PC (303-534-9000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, UMB Bank N.A.

Attorneys, Rabindra M. David and Michael Weed of the law offices of Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP (916-329-7904) appear as counsels for defendant/moving party, Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The court grants Orrick's request for judicial notice of the bond transaction documents. The court, however, cannot take judicial notice of Orrick's interpretation of those documents. (See Fremont Indem. Co. v. Fremont Gen. Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 114-115.) The documents do not conclusively or indisputably show that Orrick was not responsible for insuring that the UCC financing statements were filed. On demurrer, the court defers to plaintiff's interpretation that Orrick was responsible for the "coordination of the closing," which includes the UCC statements. The court takes plaintiff's allegations as true. Extrinsic evidence is necessary to determine the scope of Orrick's duty. The second cause of action is alleged with sufficient particularity. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR No. 9711, e-mail: lreding@sftc.org. (L&M302)
